posts.text:
A message to the Anti-America Never-Trump protesters for tomorrow.

Video

Mr. President, one of the things I like about you most is that, now, our allies take America for granted at their own peril.
 
As to my suggestion, I meant it then and I’ll repeat it now: We should consider removing U.S. bases from countries who won’t let us fly from them as we confront the world’s largest state sponsor of terrorism who has been hellbent on developing a nuclear weapon and was extremely close to achieving that goal. 
 
Spain’s refusal to allow American aircraft stationed in Spain

Checkmate 🤣


CSPAN (@cspan)

Q: "Why didn't you tell U.S. allies…about the war before attacking Iran?"
 
President Trump: "We wanted surprise. Who knows better about surprise than Japan? Why didn't you tell me about Pearl Harbor?"

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/cspan/status/2034667305318187239#m

Glad to join my friend @BillOReilly on his new podcast, We’ll Do It Live!

Tune in at 6pm. 

➡️ 
piped.video/BillOReilly


Bill O'Reilly (@BillOReilly)

.@LindseyGrahamSC is our next guest on WE’LL DO IT LIVE! 📣🇺🇸

It's available NOW on BillOReilly.com for Premium & Concierge Members, and on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and everywhere else at 6 PM ET.





— https://nitter.net/BillOReilly/status/2034721738160754770#m

I appreciate the recent joint statement by the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands and Japan reinforcing their condemnation of Iran’s outrageous behavior, and its interference with freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, viciously attacking energy infrastructure and shipping vessels.

It is imperative that we forever end the ability of the Iranian regime to terrorize the world, particularly vital international waterways, including the Strait of Hormuz. Since 1979, this

Great news for South Carolina that my good friend Senator Tim Scott intends to seek reelection in 2028. @votetimscott brings so much to the table for our state and our party. 

Thank you, Tim, for your willingness to continue to serve our wonderful state and great nation.
Great meeting today with @Moms4Liberty  South Carolina, an organization that deeply cares about the parents’ primary role in the education of their children and beyond.

Outstanding to hear South Carolina brought home two of three of the top national honors at the 2026 Young Marines Award Banquet. The Upstate Young Marines of Greenville were named Young Marine Unit of the Year, and incredibly, a member of that unit, Quinn Lawton from Estill, was named National Young Marine of the Year. Congratulations -- you all have made our state so proud.
 
@USYoungMarines is an important nonprofit that focuses on education and service for students, with a emphasis on a healt

President Trump’s decision to take the war to Kharg Island - the crown jewel of Iran’s oil and gas economy - was necessary, bold and in my view, highly effective. 

This will help shorten the war.

Video

When Iran’s future is back in the hands of its people, peace in the Middle East won’t be far behind.

God bless President @realDonaldTrump and his team for doing what others only talked about.

Video

Joe Kent is handing Democrats exactly what they need to undermine the President.

Denying an imminent threat isn’t just wrong, it’s the kind of thinking that makes America less safe.

Video

Democrats and the mainstream media won’t say what was really at stake because it proves President Trump was right.

Iran was on the path to a nuclear weapon, and President @realDonaldTrump stopped it.

Video

The terrorist attacks on October 7th were designed to keep the Middle East in chaos.

Through President @realDonaldTrump's decisive actions, real peace will be made possible once Iran's brutal regime comes to an end.

Video

You really learn a lot about the capabilities and will of your allies in difficult times like these. To those who suggest that confronting the Iranian regime and denying them a nuclear weapon which they were on the verge of acquiring is not their conflict - you’ve missed a lot since 1979.

In the nick of time, through Operation Midnight Hammer, President Trump stopped Iran from having enough weapons grade uranium to make at least 10 nuclear bombs. Iran was weeks away from achieving this, not yea

I’m here today because of the people who stood by me when it mattered most.

Their support is why I continue to serve and why I’ll never stop fighting for South Carolina.

Video

As Chairman of the Senate Budget Committee, I helped lead Republicans in delivering the largest tax and spending cuts in American history, without a single Democrat vote.

 It was an honor to secure this victory for President @realDonaldTrump, and I am ready to deliver again.

Video

As Senate Judiciary Chairman, I confirmed a record number of conservative judges and stood strong defending Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Brett Kavanaugh.

If Republicans keep the Senate, I’ll be Chairman again and will work tirelessly alongside President @realDonaldTrump to put even more conservative judges on the bench.

Video

During negotiations between Iran, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, the regime rejected the U.S. offer to provide a peaceful nuclear power program for free.  

Also during these negotiations, the Iranians made a stunning statement. They boasted to Witkoff and Kushner that they had 460 kilograms of 60% enriched uranium. There is no commercial purpose for enriching uranium to 60%.

The time to go from 60% to 90% enrichment, which is weapons-grade, is weeks, not months. 460 kilograms of uranium enri

The resignation of Joe Kent as the Director of the National Counterterrorism Center could not have come at a better time. In his statement, explaining why he’s resigning in protest, Mr. Kent is echoing the Democratic talking points, which are devoid of fact or evidence. Hopefully, the person replacing Mr. Kent will have a better understanding of the threats presented by the Iranian regime.
 
Mr. Kent said that Iran posed no imminent threat to the United States. Clearly, he did not go to work eno

Just spoke to @POTUS about our European allies’ unwillingness to provide assets to keep the Strait of Hormuz functioning, which benefits Europe far more than America. I have never heard him so angry in my life. I share that anger given what’s at stake. 

The arrogance of our allies to suggest that Iran with a nuclear weapon is of little concern and that military action to stop the ayatollah from acquiring a nuclear bomb is our problem not theirs is beyond offensive. The European approach to cont
